channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Guide to Iconic Beaches Around the World Known for Exceptional Natural Beauty
item.description: The article highlights several internationally renowned beaches that are considered worth the effort and expense of long-distance travel because of their unique landscapes, water colors, and overall a...

item.title: UK to deploy AI facial recognition to estimate age of asylum seekers at borders from next year
item.description: The UK Home Office is set to introduce an artificial intelligence facial recognition system designed to estimate the age of asylum seekers at the border, with deployment expected from next year follow...

item.title: NASA Unveils Multi-Phase Plan for Lunar South Pole Base
item.description: NASA has announced a structured, multi-phase plan to establish a permanent presence at the Moon's south pole. The program is divided into three phases. Phase one, from 2026 to 2029, will focus on robo...

item.title: Challenges and community-driven solutions for running PC games on Android using Winlator and GameHub forks
item.description: The article explores the increasingly complex landscape of running Windows PC games on Android smartphones, focusing on tools like Winlator and the controversial GameHub ecosystem. The author initiall...

item.title: Carlsen defeats Gukesh in Norway Chess 2026 Round 4 as Indian women players face setbacks
item.description: In Round 4 of Norway Chess 2026 held in Oslo, World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen registered a crucial victory over World Champion D. Gukesh in a closely fought classical game. The contest remained intense thr...

item.title: Becerra leads California governor primary as Hilton and Steyer compete for second place
item.description: A recent poll by UC Berkeley’s Institute of Governmental Studies, co-sponsored by the Los Angeles Times, shows former Biden Cabinet secretary Xavier Becerra leading California's highly competitive Jun...

item.title: Weekend guide to events and activities in Ottawa for May 29–31
item.description: The article from CTV News Ottawa provides a curated guide to activities and events taking place in Canada’s capital over the weekend of May 29 to 31. It is designed to help residents and visitors make...

item.title: Marcos dismisses rumours of DILG leadership change, reaffirms trust in Jonvic Remulla
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. has publicly dismissed circulating rumours suggesting that Interior and Local Government Secretary Jonvic Remulla would be replaced, reaffirming his confidenc...

item.title: Shekhawat calls for stronger state action to preserve British-era heritage structures in India
item.description: Union Culture and Tourism Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat has stressed the urgent need for better preservation of British-era heritage structures across India, urging state governments to take more ...

item.title: Kithure Kindiki Clarifies Use of 'My Lady' While Addressing Chief Justice Martha Koome at Prayer Breakfast
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki brought a light-hearted moment during the National Prayer Breakfast after he took time to explain his use of the phrase “My Lady” when referring to Chief Justice Marth...

item.title: Nigeria’s security crisis remains a major challenge under Tinubu administration despite military gains
item.description: As President Bola Ahmed Tinubu approaches the latter part of his first tenure, security continues to be one of the biggest problems facing his government. Since he assumed office in May 2023, his admi...

item.title: CSOs report nearly 20,000 deaths and over 12,000 abductions in Nigeria over three years
item.description: A coalition of civil society organisations (CSOs) in Nigeria has raised alarm over the level of insecurity in the country, stating that about 19,980 people have been killed and 12,362 others abducted ...

item.title: Yanga and Simba, Azam battle for CAF Champions League and Confederation Cup qualification in Tanzania Premier League
item.description: Tanzania’s top football giants Young Africans (Yanga) and Simba SC have already secured places in next season’s CAF inter-club competitions, but the exact tournament each team will play in is still un...

item.title: Studio Portraits of African American Women in the Late 19th Century Showcasing Elegance and Resilience
item.description: The article highlights a collection of 40 studio portraits of African American women taken during the late 19th century, a period marked by the post-Reconstruction era in the United States. Despite wi...

item.title: Marcos expresses opposition to Senate online voting proposal
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., formerly a senator himself, voiced his opposition to a proposed amendment allowing online voting in the Philippine Senate. Speaking during a press briefing in Tokyo on ...

item.title: Tanzania launches investor drive for redevelopment of Msasani and Sinza urban areas
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has officially started a major initiative to attract investors for the redevelopment of Msasani and Sinza areas in Dar es Salaam, aiming to modernise deteriorating urban set...

item.title: Serena Williams linked to possible doubles return at Queen’s Club
item.description: Reports suggest that tennis legend Serena Williams could be preparing for a return to competitive tennis, potentially in a doubles event at Queen’s Club in London next month. The 23-time Grand Slam ch...

item.title: Review: aespa's second studio album 'Lemonade' mixes confidence and inconsistency
item.description: In their second studio album 'Lemonade', released on 29th May 2026 by SM Entertainment, K-pop group aespa continues to navigate the challenges of maintaining momentum after a string of major hits. The...

item.title: Kerala Opposition Leader Comments on Partial Rendition of Vande Mataram in Assembly
item.description: Kerala Leader of Opposition and former Chief Minister, Pinarayi Vijayan, has expressed his disapproval over the full rendition of the national song 'Vande Mataram' during official ceremonies in Kerala...

item.title: Canadian officials remind World Cup fans that tickets do not guarantee entry into Canada
item.description: Canadian border authorities are warning international football fans that having a ticket to FIFA World Cup matches in Canada does not guarantee entry into the country. With thousands of supporters exp...
